docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions
docs.text: Answers to the most common questions about SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor.

docs.title: Which file formats are supported?
docs.text: The software supports WIMU® datasets exported in Microsoft Excel (.xlsx) and Comma-Separated Values (.csv) formats.

docs.title: Can multiple files be processed?
docs.text: Yes. The application supports single-file, multi-file, and batch processing.

docs.title: Is validation mandatory?
docs.text: No. Validation is optional and is recommended when reference datasets are available.

docs.title: Which reports are generated?
docs.text:
Depending on the selected workflow, the software can generate:

  • Excel workbooks.

  • Word technical reports.

  • Validation reports.

  • Processing logs.

docs.title: Where can I report an issue?
docs.text: Issues and feature requests can be submitted through the project's GitHub Issue Tracker.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Get started with SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor in a few minutes.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor provides a standardized workflow for extracting, validating, and reporting WIMU® performance data.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Select one or more WIMU® datasets.
  2. Extract the available variables.
  3. Standardize the dataset structure.
  4. Validate the processed data.
  5. Generate the reports.
  6. Review the results.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: Component Requirement Operating System Windows 10/11 Python 3.11 or later Microsoft Excel Optional

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Installation
  • User Guide
  • Technical Reports

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Install SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor and prepare the software for use.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: Component Requirement Operating System Windows 10/11 Python 3.11 or later RAM 4 GB minimum Disk Space 500 MB Microsoft Excel Optional

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Clone the repository:

git clone https://github.com/SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor.git
cd SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor

Create a virtual environment:

python -m venv .venv

Activate the environment:

.venv\Scripts\activate

Install the required packages:

pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
Start the application:

python main.py

The main menu should open without errors.

docs.title: Update
docs.text:
Update the local repository and dependencies:

git pull
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Technical Report TR-001 — User Guide
docs.text: Describe the standard use of SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Provide a concise reference for the complete workflow, from data selection to report generation.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Select the input datasets.
  2. Choose the processing mode.
  3. Extract and standardize the data.
  4. Validate the processed variables.
  5. Generate the reports.
  6. Archive the results.

docs.title: Input Data
docs.text:
Supported formats:

  • Microsoft Excel (.xlsx)
  • Comma-Separated Values (.csv)

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
• Standardized Excel datasets.
  • Word technical reports.
  • Validation reports.
  • Processing logs.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Preserve the original datasets.
  • Process complete exports.
  • Archive reports with the processed data.
  • Record the software version.

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
• Windows is the recommended platform.
  • Input files must follow the supported WIMU® export structure.

docs.title: Technical Report TR-002 — Processing Workflow
docs.text: Document the workflow used by SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor to transform WIMU® datasets into standardized scientific outputs.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Describe each processing stage from data import to report generation.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
WIMU® Dataset
      │
      ▼
Import
      │
      ▼
Extraction
      │
      ▼
Standardization
      │
      ▼
Quality Control
      │
      ▼
Validation
      │
      ▼
Report Generation

docs.title: Processing Stages
docs.text: Stage Description Import Load one or more WIMU® datasets. Extraction Read supported variables. Standardization Apply common variable names and formats. Quality Control Verify data integrity and consistency. Validation Compare results with reference data (optional). Reporting Generate standardized outputs.

docs.title: Generated Outputs
docs.text:
• Excel datasets.
  • Word reports.
  • Validation reports.
  • Processing logs.

docs.text:
• TR-001 User Guide

docs.title: Technical Report TR-003 — Variable Dictionary
docs.text: Describe the variables extracted and standardized by SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Provide a reference for all variables generated during data extraction and standardization.

docs.title: Variable Categories
docs.text: Category Description Participant Information Player and participant data. Session Information Session metadata. External Load Performance and movement metrics. Internal Load Physiological response variables. Positioning Data GNSS positioning variables. Derived Variables Calculated metrics generated by the software.

docs.title: Variable Definition
docs.text:
Each variable includes:

  • Name
  • Description
  • Unit
  • Data Type
  • Source

docs.title: Standardization
docs.text: Variable names and formats are standardized to ensure compatibility across datasets and software versions.

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: The standardized variable dictionary is included in the generated Excel datasets and technical reports.

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• TR-002 Processing Workflow
  • TR-004 Validation Protocol
  • TR-005 Output Specification

docs.title: Technical Report TR-004 — Validation Protocol
docs.text: Describe the scientific protocol used to validate the outputs generated by SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Define the methodology used to assess the accuracy, consistency, and reproducibility of the extracted datasets.

docs.title: Validation Strategy
docs.text: Validation compares the software outputs with the corresponding reference datasets exported from WIMU® software.

docs.title: Validation Workflow
docs.text:
Script Output
      │
      ▼
Reference Dataset
      │
      ▼
Variable Matching
      │
      ▼
Statistical Analysis
      │
      ▼
Validation Report

docs.title: Validation Metrics
docs.text: Metric Description Mean Difference (MD) Average difference between methods. Mean Absolute Error (MAE) Mean absolute error. Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) Overall prediction error. Pearson Correlation Linear association between methods. Intraclass Correlation Coefficient (ICC) Agreement between measurements. Bland–Altman Analysis Agreement and systematic bias.

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text:
• Variable comparison tables.
  • Agreement statistics.
  • Validation summary.
  • Technical recommendations.

docs.title: Related Documentation
docs.text:
• TR-002 Processing Workflow
  • TR-003 Variable Dictionary
  • TR-005 Output Specification

docs.title: Technical Report TR-005 — Output Specification
docs.text: Define the structure and content of all files generated by SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Describe every output generated after data processing.

docs.title: Generated Files
docs.text: Output Description Excel Workbook Standardized datasets ready for analysis. Word Report Technical summary of the processing results. Validation Report Concordance analysis with the reference software. Processing Log Execution summary and diagnostic information.

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text: Generated outputs are organized using a standardized directory structure to simplify management and ensure reproducibility.

docs.title: File Naming
docs.text:
Each output filename includes:

  • Project name.
  • Report type.
  • Processing date.
  • Software version.

docs.title: Recommendations
docs.text:
• Preserve all generated outputs.
  • Keep original datasets unchanged.
  • Archive reports with the processed datasets.
  • Record the software version used.

docs.title: Technical Report TR-006 — Software Architecture
docs.text: Describe the architecture of SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: Provide an overview of the software structure and the interaction between its main modules.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
WIMU® Data
     │
     ▼
Data Import
     │
     ▼
Extraction Engine
     │
     ▼
Processing Engine
     │
     ▼
Validation Engine
     │
     ▼
Reporting Engine
     │
     ▼
Excel / Word Outputs

docs.title: Software Modules
docs.text: Module Function Data Import Loads WIMU® datasets. Extraction Engine Reads supported variables. Processing Engine Standardizes datasets and variables. Validation Engine Compares outputs with reference datasets. Reporting Engine Generates Excel and Word reports.

docs.title: Design Principles
docs.text:
• Modular architecture.
  • Independent processing stages.
  • Standardized outputs.
  • Scientific reproducibility.
  • Easy maintenance and scalability.

docs.title: User Guide
docs.text: Learn how to use SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor to process WIMU® datasets and generate standardized outputs.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The software follows a simple and reproducible workflow from data selection to report generation.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
1. Select the input datasets.
  2. Configure the processing options.
  3. Process the selected files.
  4. Validate the extracted data.
  5. Generate the reports.
  6. Archive the results.

docs.title: Menus
docs.text: Navigate the application through a simple menu designed to support the complete processing workflow.

docs.title: Main Menu
docs.text:
The main menu provides access to the core functions of the application.

Option Description Process Single File Process a single dataset. Process Multiple Files Process multiple datasets. Batch Processing Process all available datasets. Validation Compare results with the reference software. Exit Close the application.

docs.title: Processing
docs.text: Each option guides the user through file selection, processing, validation, and report generation.

docs.title: Recommendations
docs.text:
• Verify the selected input files.
  • Process complete datasets whenever possible.
  • Save results in a dedicated output folder.
  • Use the latest stable release.

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text: Understand how the project is organized and where each component is located.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The project follows a modular structure that separates source code, documentation, datasets, validation resources, and generated reports.

Directory Description src/ Application source code. data/ Input and output datasets. docs/ User and technical documentation. validation/ Validation resources and reference datasets. reports/ Generated reports. assets/ Images, logos, and documentation resources. tests/ Software testing modules.

docs.title: Directory Layout
docs.text: Each directory has a specific purpose and should remain unchanged to ensure compatibility with future releases.

docs.title: Recommendations
docs.text:
• Preserve the original project structure.
  • Store datasets in the appropriate folders.
  • Keep reports together with the processed data.
  • Avoid renaming project directories.

docs.text: Generate standardized outputs for scientific analysis, validation, and documentation.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The software generates reports that summarize the processed datasets, quality control results, and validation outcomes.

docs.title: Available Reports
docs.text: Report Description Excel Workbook Standardized datasets ready for analysis. Word Report Technical summary of the processing results. Validation Report Concordance analysis with the reference software. Processing Log Execution summary and diagnostic information.

docs.title: Report Contents
docs.text:
Reports include:

  • Project information.
  • Processing summary.
  • Processed datasets.
  • Variable overview.
  • Quality control results.
  • Validation results.

docs.title: Recommendations
docs.text:
• Preserve all generated reports.
  • Archive reports with the original datasets.
  • Record the software version used.
  • Retain reports as supporting material for reproducible research.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Resolve the most common issues encountered during data processing and report generation.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Most issues are related to unsupported input files, missing dependencies, or incorrect project configuration.

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text: Issue Solution Input file cannot be opened Verify that the file exists and is not being used by another application. No data extracted Confirm that the selected file is a supported WIMU® export. Validation failed Ensure that both comparison files belong to the same dataset. Report generation failed Verify that the output folder is writable and that report files are closed.

docs.title: Before Requesting Support
docs.text:
Verify that:

  • The latest software version is installed.
  • All required dependencies are available.
  • Input files are complete and valid.
  • The issue can be reproduced.

docs.title: Support Information
docs.text:
Include the following information when reporting an issue:

  • Software version.
  • Operating system.
  • Description of the problem.
  • Steps to reproduce the issue.
  • Error message, if available.

docs.title: Processing Workflows
docs.text: Process WIMU® datasets using a standardized and reproducible workflow.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The software follows a sequential workflow from data extraction to report generation, ensuring consistent processing across all datasets.

docs.title: Workflow
docs.text:
Input Files
      │
      ▼
Data Extraction
      │
      ▼
Data Standardization
      │
      ▼
Quality Control
      │
      ▼
Validation
      │
      ▼
Report Generation

docs.title: Processing Steps
docs.text:
1. Select one or more WIMU® datasets.
  2. Extract the available variables.
  3. Standardize the dataset structure.
  4. Perform automatic quality control.
  5. Validate the results against reference data (optional).
  6. Generate the output reports.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text: Scientific validation resources for SportsLabResearch-WIMU-DataExtractor.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text: This section documents the comparison between the extraction script and SPRO reference outputs.

docs.title: Available Resources
docs.text:
• Validation overview.
  • Validation protocol.
  • Validation results.
  • Validation report in PDF.

docs.title: Main Findings
docs.text:
• High overall agreement between tools.
  • Excellent concordance for position, GPS speed, and acceleration variables.
  • Isolated discrepancies in some gyroscope and magnetometer variables.
  • Review of units, scaling, and reference frames is recommended for discrepant variables.

docs.title: Validation Report
docs.text: Open the validation report

docs.title: Script Extraction vs SPRO
docs.text:
This report summarizes the concordance analysis between the extraction script and SPRO reference outputs.

Key indicators include ICC, MAE, RMSE, and overall agreement by variable group.

Open PDF report
